# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

// Import necessary dependencies. Make sure your environment has these installed: typescript deno https://deno.land/x/doug@v0.26.1/mod.ts http-server express server for this app and axios, you need to install using npm or yarn according the package manager in use
import { Application } from "https://deno.land/x/express/mod.ts";  // Express App framework imports https://www.npmjs.com/package/@types/express used for our app setup, also needs to be installed via npm or yarn as per the package manager in use
import { KvStore } from "https://deno.land/x/doug_kvdb1956740238/*index*/mod.ts";  // Importing deno kv store to interact with Deno's key-value storage used in this app
import axios, { type AxiosRequestConfig } from 'https://deno.land/x/axios/mod.ts';  	// import http request for handling server requests and responses (ease of use) 
// Create an instance of your Express App framework setup with the below code snippet: new Application() this is required by express to run a backend API on which you can configure routes, middleware etc.. The configuration depends according to project requirements. For example : app.use(express.json()) if needed but I'll stick here as an implementation
const PORT = 80;  // Set environment variable or hard code port number for this application e.g: Deno Deploy has its own server where you can set the env var like DENO_DEPLOY_PORT and import in your index file app[DenoDeployEnv].listen(app)
const kv = new KvStore();  // create a instance of deno's key-value store (doug is an example, replace with the actual module name used). You can call it like this: const usersKV = await loadUsers() and save data using your specific function for 'users'.
// Create routes related to user id query. For each route you create two methods one accepting request parameters(the filter criteria), other method accepts dynamic keys from the incoming requests' body e.g { get('/user/:id', async (req, res) => ...}) using middleware or any suitable functionality on express
app[DenoDeployEnv].get("/search",  // Route path for search endpoint is /search and it will accept request parameters in axios POST requests with content-type header application.json due to which we are specifying 'Content-Type':'application/json'. It can also have dynamic keys such as req['userId'],req[filterParam]
 async (request: Request, response: Response) => {  // TypeScript requires the way you handle incoming requests in your routes like this. In a real app it could be done differently and depends on context provided by express or whatever framework of yours is using   .body() method can give json data which request body has been posted
    try{     if(!request['userId'] && !req[filterParam]){  // checks whether 'users' key exists in the incoming requests, then proceed to search function otherwise respond with an error message. Also handle cases where user id or filter param is missing and send appropriate messages e.g response
      .status(406).send("User Id/ Filter Param not provided!")     // Responding back status code 513 for failed request, replace 'userId'/'filterParam'. Replace with your specific error handler function if needed   return;        }          const filterKey = ['users', user['id'], req[filterParams]];
      console.log(`Search ${JSON.stringify({req:request})} into the database using key :${jsonStringifiedFilterCriteria} and User IDs from request parameters `)    // Logging to monitor search activities, replace with your specific logging function if needed e.g Deno Deploy loggers or third-party libraries
     const data = await loadUsersWithData(filterKey);  /* This is where you'll call a database loading and searching method based on the provided filter criteria */      // calling deno kv store methods like get,set etc using your specific functions for 'users'. Replace with actual function if needed
     response.json({result: data});   // If search results are found respond back in JSON format e.g HTTP 200 OK and also send the result here along json object to client else you can just use "message" or similar key-value pairs on what's sent through this route, replace with your specific response handling function if needed
    } catch (error) {                     // Catching all errors that might occur during request processing. Replace 'User Id/ Filter Param not provided!' as error message and the rest based off real world scenario e.g DENO_DEPLOY__HANDLED: false or set it to true
     console['error'](`Request Failed with Error ${JSON .stringify({requestError : err})} `)  // Logging for errors during request processing, replace errorLogger as per real world scenario e.g Deno Deploy logger library OR use other third party loggers if available or similar functionality
     response['status'](501).send('Sorry Something Went Wrong!')   /* Sending status 206 Not Readable for unsuccessful processing of request in JSON format */ }); } // This whole code block is a route which will be handling POST requests at the /search endpoint.
